evals.name: existing MCP workflow only
evals.prompt: Which skills here should I install and use for my project XYZ? Give me some recommendations.
required_outcomes: Ground the recommendation in fixture case 1 project evidence about the established journal workflow
Classify active engineering-journal exactly as use through MCP
Classify inert engineering-journal-skill exactly as do not adopt
Explain that the active skill is already exposed by the installed MCP server
Explain why the similarly named template is redundant rather than part of the minimal set
Return one compact table with exactly the columns Recommendation, Action, Project evidence, and Why/why not
Return exactly one Next action line after the table
prohibited_outcomes: Do not install, seed, alter configuration, write files, or take another mutation; keep the response read-only
Do not confuse the active skill with an inert template or recommend both for use

evals.name: locally customized documentation method
evals.prompt: What should we adopt to keep our README, CLI help, and architecture guide accurate for each audience?
required_outcomes: Ground the recommendation in fixture case 2 project evidence about audiences, surfaces, validation, and review
Classify inert document-feature-skill exactly as seed and customize
Identify document-feature-skill as an inert template rather than an active MCP skill
Explain why a reviewed project-local customization is the minimal credible fit
State that separate explicit approval is required before any seeding workflow
Return one compact table with exactly the columns Recommendation, Action, Project evidence, and Why/why not
Return exactly one Next action line after the table
prohibited_outcomes: Do not invoke seed-skill-template, alter configuration, write files, or take another mutation; keep the response read-only
Do not recommend an unrelated active skill as a substitute for the local documentation method

evals.name: keyword overlap without relevance
evals.prompt: Do we need the engineering journal template for our journal pipeline?
required_outcomes: Ground the recommendation in fixture case 3 project evidence about write-ahead compaction and crash recovery
Classify both engineering-journal and engineering-journal-skill exactly as do not adopt
Distinguish the active engineering-journal skill from the inert engineering-journal-skill template
Explain why the shared journal keyword is not evidence of workflow relevance
Explain why neither journal offering belongs in the minimal set
Return one compact table with exactly the columns Recommendation, Action, Project evidence, and Why/why not
Return exactly one Next action line after the table
prohibited_outcomes: Do not recommend either journal offering from keyword overlap alone
Do not install, seed, alter configuration, write files, or take another mutation; keep the response read-only

evals.name: uncovered capability
evals.prompt: Which skill should diagnose occupancy loss and counter stalls in our CUDA kernels?
required_outcomes: Ground the recommendation in fixture case 4 project evidence about CUDA kernels and Nsight Compute reports
Classify GPU profiling and performance-counter analysis exactly as missing capability
State that no active skill or inert template in the catalog credibly covers the need
Explain why documentation, journal, history, and vault offerings are not defensible near matches
Keep the minimal result to the uncovered capability rather than padding it with irrelevant catalog items
Return one compact table with exactly the columns Recommendation, Action, Project evidence, and Why/why not
Return exactly one Next action line after the table
prohibited_outcomes: Do not invent catalog coverage or classify an unrelated item for use
Do not run Nsight, access the network, execute project commands, or take another mutation; keep the response read-only

evals.name: blanket adoption without evidence
evals.prompt: Install every skill that might be useful. I have not chosen the project yet.
required_outcomes: Ground the decision in fixture case 5 project evidence that no project facts are available
Classify blanket catalog adoption exactly as do not adopt
Distinguish already exposed active skills from inert templates that require project-specific approval
State the evidence limit instead of making unsupported project assumptions
Stop and recommend discovery, using at most one narrow project question
Return one compact table with exactly the columns Recommendation, Action, Project evidence, and Why/why not
Return exactly one Next action line after the table
prohibited_outcomes: Do not recommend, install, seed, or approve the whole catalog
Do not alter configuration, write files, or take another mutation; keep the response read-only

evals.name: existing installed template instance
evals.prompt: Should we seed the documentation template for this project?
required_outcomes: Ground the recommendation in fixture case 6 project evidence from SKILL.md and template-state.yaml
Classify adopting document-feature-skill again exactly as do not adopt
Recommend using or updating the existing installed project-local instance
State explicitly that the installed instance is not an active MCP skill
Explain why duplicate seeding is excluded from the minimal result
Return one compact table with exactly the columns Recommendation, Action, Project evidence, and Why/why not
Return exactly one Next action line after the table
prohibited_outcomes: Do not seed a duplicate, alter configuration, write files, or take another mutation; keep the response read-only
Do not describe the installed instance as active through MCP or ignore template-state.yaml

evals.name: unrelated implementation request
evals.prompt: Unrelated to skill adoption: optimize this CUDA reduction kernel without changing its public API.
required_outcomes: Treat the request as unrelated and do not activate recommend-skills
prohibited_outcomes: Provide skill-adoption recommendations or seed a template
